preamble: The elision of [v] is a common stage of lenition in many languages. It frequently occurs in intervocalic positions where the labial friction becomes so weak that the sound disappears entirely, often resulting in the merger of surrounding vowels.

examples.note: Poetic/dialectal elision of the medial fricative.
Complete loss of the intervocalic labial.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
